title: MySQL reports a error when group by has a lateral subquery.
description:
It reports error message: Duplicate column name 'a'
How to repeat:
following SQL:

create table t1(a int);
select (select ti1.a as c0
                from
                  t1 as ti1,
                  lateral (select to1.a as c00,
                        to1.a as c11
                      from
                        t1 as ti2) as subq_0 limit 1) as c1
      from
        t1 as to1 group by 1;

Note, If remove 'group by 1' from above SQL, the query can be executed successfully.
poc:
select (select ti1.a as c0
from
t1 as ti1,
lateral (select to1.a as c00,
to1.a as c11
from
t1 as ti2) as subq_0 limit 1) as c1
from
t1 as to1 group by 1;
key_bug_features: Group by with lateral subquery causes duplicate column error.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(b varchar(255), c int, d float);
INSERT INTO t2(b, c, d) VALUES ('test', 1, 1.1), ('example', 2, 2.2);
SELECT (SELECT ti2.b AS c0 FROM t2 AS ti2, LATERAL (SELECT ti2.c AS c00, ti2.d AS c11 FROM t2 AS ti3) AS subq_1 LIMIT 1) AS c1 FROM t2 AS to2 GROUP BY 1;
CREATE TABLE t3(e date, f timestamp);
INSERT INTO t3(e, f) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
